spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates the headline capability the spec promises: navigator.storage.estimate() returns identical quota in normal and incognito. Aligned with the chromestatus motivation.

interactive.rationale: Real controls drive the demo (buttons, selects/inputs, live script) — well past the static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb on the feature index closely matches what the concept page delivers — title, lede, and interactive surface line up.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path is solid and several failure modes are probed, but the page misses a couple of the edge cases the spec calls out (see open questions).

references.rationale: No see-also section and no chromestatus.com/feature link on this concept page; relies on the parent feature index for the canonical link. The self-heal script needs it embedded on every page.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex creeping in, button colours and contrast appear within AA.

openQuestions.title: Add other known Incognito-detection signals to the simulator
openQuestions.detail: Quota is one vector. The simulator could expand to compare against FileSystem API access, sw cache eviction speed, IndexedDB transactions/sec — to show that the quota fix is targeted, not universal.

openQuestions.title: Embed the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link in this concept page
openQuestions.detail: The self-heal recovery script relies on every concept page carrying the canonical chromestatus link in a see-also or references block. Add it: https://chromestatus.com/feature/5143175657291776

summary: Adjustable RAM/disk/threshold replay of the attacker decision logic. Main gaps: Add other known Incognito-detection signals to the simulator; Embed the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link in this concept page.
